claim: AlphaFoldExtractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor to fetch AlphaFold-DB predicted structures and extract 9 structural descriptors per Syn3A CDS. Cache parquet in commit ebbfdff is REGISTERED BUT EMPTY (455 rows, af_has_structure=0 for every row, all other 8 columns NaN) because JCVI-Syn3A (taxid 2144189, 'synthetic bacterium JCVI-Syn3A') is not indexed in UniProt and AFDB keys are UniProt accessions. Four UniProt query routes (direct search, xref:embl-CP016816, proteome name, organism_id stream) each returned zero rows. The parquet is deliberately tracked by the manifest so Session 15 can swap in real values via a curated UniProt mapping (e.g. from the Luthey-Schulten 4DWCM supplement) without touching the FeatureRegistry contract.

value.name: alphafold_db
value.cache_content_status: empty by design: JCVI-Syn3A not indexed in UniProt; every row has af_has_structure=0.0 and all 8 numeric columns NaN

source_detail: Session 14. Verifiable by reading the module + running 6 sandbox-safe tests. Structures themselves come from the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database (EBI-EMBL) — Varadi et al. 2024 Nucleic Acids Research — fetched on demand by the extractor at run time. The AFDB structures are NOT registered as a first-class memory_bank source; they are external read-only data that the extractor pulls via HTTP.

context.commit_range: TBD — filled in once Session 14 push lands.

caveats: Cache parquet populated in commit ebbfdff but CONTENT IS EMPTY: 455 rows of NaN + af_has_structure=0.0 for every CDS. Reason documented in the claim: JCVI-Syn3A (taxid 2144189) is a synthetic organism not indexed in UniProt. AFDB URLs key on UniProt accessions.

The extractor's code path was exercised end-to-end on Colab — the zero-structures outcome reflects data availability, not an extraction bug. The extractor's retry / 404 / biopython-parse paths remain correct for any organism whose proteins ARE in UniProt.

Session 15 pivot: SUPERSEDED FOR SYN3A by esmfold_extractor. The Luthey-Schulten 4DWCM repo was confirmed to not carry a NCBI-to-UniProt cross-reference CSV (listed 14 files in its input_data/, none is a mapping). Combined with sandbox-side network allowlist blocking rest.uniprot.org and www.ebi.ac.uk, live UniProt mapping from Syn3A AVX* accessions is doubly blocked. notebooks/populate_tier1_cache.ipynb cell 6 now calls ESMFoldExtractor instead of AlphaFoldExtractor. The AlphaFold module remains in the repo for any future organism whose UniProt entries ARE accessible; for Syn3A itself, esmfold_v1 is the path forward.

Secondary-structure classification in this extractor is a torsion-bin proxy for DSSP; when the cache is populated with real structures in a future session, helix / sheet / coil fractions should be treated as ordinal and ~10-15% noisier than DSSP.
No MCC measurement is claimed. A follow-up measured fact will document any detector built on top of these features.
